game_name: Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter

design_philosophy.core_loop: Pilot selection → Squadron role assignment → Multiplayer dogfighting across symmetric factions (Rebel X-Wings vs Imperial TIE Fighters)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Asymmetric craft balance and positioning in deep-space 3D combat requiring constant awareness of all friendly and enemy craft positions
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Commanding a starfighter squadron in iconic Star Wars space battles, experiencing both sides of the Galactic Civil War
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately designed as a multiplayer-first experience with minimal single-player narrative; rejected story-driven campaign in base game as design choice (lead designer Wessman explicitly stated 'we didn't really need it'). Emphasis on symmetric competitive play over narrative immersion. Later rectified via Balance of Power expansion due to player demand.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects craft type and squadron role per mission, determining combat capabilities and responsibilities in multiplayer matches

progression.upgrade_system: Pilot and mission selection system that tracks player points and awards; craft selection provides mechanical progression through access to different starfighter types
progression.unlock_mechanism: Completion of missions and accumulation of pilot rating/points unlocks new craft options (B-wings added in Balance of Power expansion)
replayability_hooks: Multiplayer competitive modes (free-for-all, team-based, cooperative) for up to 8 players
Two parallel campaigns in Balance of Power with alternate endings (Rebel 'Spreading the Rebellion' vs Imperial 'Task Force - Vengeance')
Craft selection variety enabling different playstyles

world_building_techniques: Parallel narrative campaigns (Balance of Power expansion) showing same events from opposing faction perspectives
Mission-specific objectives tied to Galactic Civil War events (base evacuations, Super Star Destroyer assaults, factory destruction)
Faction-specific cutscenes with alternate endings based on player choice (Rebel vs Imperial campaign)

notable_bosses: Super Star Destroyer Vengeance (Rebel campaign climax)

faction_indicators: Rebel faction: X-Wings, Y-Wings, B-Wings (added in expansion)
Imperial faction: TIE Fighters, TIE Bombers, Assault Gunboats
danger_signals: Asymmetric craft capabilities requiring team coordination
Deep-space setting necessitates constant awareness of all player positions
Squadron role assignment creates implicit team hierarchies
environmental_storytelling: Mission objectives reflect galactic events (base evacuations, capital ship operations)
Parallel campaign narratives show consequences of same battles from opposing viewpoints
